stem:
M(t) = 800(1.6)t

The equation above models the number of members, M, of a club t years after the club opens. Of the following, which equation models the number of members of the club q semester years (half-years) after the club opens?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. Since q counts half-year periods, t = q/2 years. Substituting into M = 800(1.6)t gives M = 800(1.6)q/2 = 800(1.61/2)q = 800(√1.6)q.

Choice A treats one semester as two years (the reverse conversion). Choice B incorrectly scales the base by 2. Choice D incorrectly halves the initial value.
